description: The chart only shows data from 2010 to 2022, a period where China's CO₂ emissions per capita rose modestly from about 5.9 to 7.5 metric tons — suggesting a gradual, flattening increase. This hides the dramatic surge from 1975 to 2010 (1.3 to 5.9 metric tons), making China's long-term emissions growth trajectory appear far less steep than it actually was over the full ~50-year period. The title still implies the chart covers all available data for China.
